2604.00757.title: IWP: Token Pruning as Implicit Weight Pruning in Large Vision Language Models
2604.00757.paper_summary: Large Vision Language Models show impressive performance across image and video understanding tasks, yet their computational cost grows rapidly with the number of visual tokens. Existing token pruning methods mitigate this issue through empirical approaches while overlooking the internal mechanism of attention. In this paper, we propose a novel training free token pruning framework grounded in the dual form perspective of attention. We reformulate attention as an implicit linear layer whose weight matrix is the sum of rank 1 outer products, each generated by a single token's key value pair. Token pruning thus reduces to selecting an optimal subset of these rank 1 updates that best approximates the original dual weight matrix. Extending this perspective to standard softmax attention in LVLMs, we derive a novel metric quantifying both a token's information magnitude and information duplication. To efficiently select the subset with the proposed metric, we introduce Progressive Chunked Maximal Marginal Relevance. Extensive experiments demonstrate that our method achieves a better trade off between performance and efficiency, while providing another perspective on existing pruning approaches.
2604.00757.paper_summary_zh: 大型視覺語言模型在圖像和視頻理解任務中展現了令人印象深刻的表現，然而其計算成本隨著視覺標記的數量迅速增長。現有的標記修剪方法通過經驗方法來緩解這一問題，但忽略了注意力的內部機制。在本文中，我們提出了一種新穎的無需訓練的標記修剪框架，基於注意力的雙重形式視角。我們將注意力重新表述為一個隱式線性層，其權重矩陣是秩為1的外積的總和，每個外積由單個標記的鍵值對生成。因此，標記修剪簡化為選擇這些秩為1的更新的最佳子集，以最佳地近似原始的雙重權重矩陣。將這一視角擴展到LVLM中的標準softmax注意力，我們推導出一種新指標，量化標記的信息大小和信息重複性。為了有效地選擇具有所提指標的子集，我們引入了漸進式分塊最大邊際相關性。大量實驗表明，我們的方法在性能和效率之間實現了更好的權衡，同時提供了對現有修剪方法的另一種視角。
2604.00757.author: Dong-Jae Lee et.al.
2604.00757.authors: Dong-Jae Lee, Sunghyun Baek, Junmo Kim
